summary: Large Samus Grapple item decompilation pass. The PR replaced many placeholders in itsamusgrapple.c with typed implementations and near-matches, added/typed Samus grapple attribute and item-var structures, improved function prototypes in itsamusgrapple.h, and refined a few ftSamus data-attribute and motion-var fields. The author explicitly noted many near-matches and duplicated code, suggesting that better semantic organization of the grapple behavior would help resolve remaining matches. decomp.dev reported +3336 matched code bytes, +16 matched data bytes, 9 new full matches, and 13 substantial unmatched-item improvements.
what_changed: Implemented a large amount of src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c, adding typed code for grapple setup, animation/state sync helpers, motion physics callbacks, collision/retraction helpers, and item/fighter interaction routines.
Added itSamusGrappleAttributes in itCharItems.h with many float tuning fields plus HSD_Joint, HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint pointer fields used by grapple article setup.
Corrected itSamusGrapple_ItemVars fields x0 and x4 from f32 to ItemLink* and used the samusgrapple item-var union member where the old code had referenced linkhookshot.x0.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes in itsamusgrapple.h with typed signatures using ItemLink*, Vec3*, itSamusGrappleAttributes*, Fighter*, Fighter_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, Item*, s32, bool, and f32.
Typed ftSamus data attributes at offsets xA0 and xB0 as s32 and xCC as f32, and added a new ftSamus_State7Vars motion-var union member with a TODO for the proper state name.

Added many dependencies needed by the reconstructed code, including ftCommon, ftCo_AirCatch, ftCo_CliffJump, ftCo_DamageFall, ft_081B, ftcliffcommon, mpcoll, mplib, lbvector, baselib/jobj, baselib/random, and efsync.
decomp.dev reported full matches for it_802B9CE8, it_802BACC4, it_802B743C, it_802B900C, it_802B91C4, fn_802B8524, fn_802B8814, it_802BA2D8, and .sdata.

decomp.dev reported major near-match improvements for fn_802B805C, fn_802B8D38, it_802BA3BC, fn_802B7E34, it_802BA760, fn_802B895C, fn_802B8B54, it_802B9FD4, fn_802B8384, it_802BA5DC, fn_802B8684, it_802BA194, and .sdata2.
smart_moves: Typed the Samus grapple item vars as ItemLink* before building out logic around linked grapple segments, making the later code much more expressive than raw f32/UNK usage.
Introduced a dedicated itSamusGrappleAttributes struct so article special attributes could be accessed consistently for physics constants, joint resources, and animation-resource arrays.

Factored repeated matching-sensitive behavior into static inline helpers such as samus_grapple_fighter_compare, samus_grapple_setup_pos, samus_grapple_anim, samus_grapple_state_sync, samus_grapple_init_link, and samus_grapple_calc_grav.
Kept local helper names semantic where possible while retaining address-based names for still-unknown functions, preserving searchability and decomp continuity.
Used exact-looking prototype recovery in the public header rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, which enables downstream work to call and reason about these routines safely.
Recognized repeated animation-loading patterns for the three grapple article types in it_802B743C and mapped the corresponding joint/anim/matanim/shapeanim attribute fields.
Preserved matching tactics such as explicit NULL branches, temporary scopes, stack padding, and local-variable layout hints where needed for near-matches.
decomp_lessons: For highly duplicated item code, semantic helper extraction can improve both readability and matching work, but the PR author warned that remaining near-matches likely need deeper behavioral understanding rather than mechanical rewriting alone.
Typing item-specific xDD4 item vars early pays off: once x0/x4 were ItemLink* rather than generic/floating fields, linked-chain traversal, animation, collision, and cleanup logic became much clearer.
Article special-attribute structs often mix physics constants and resource pointers; recovering the resource-pointer tail can unlock HSD_JObjLoadJoint and HSD_JObjAddAnimAll setup code.
When an item is a fighter accessory, matching work must coordinate item state, Fighter motion IDs, fighter callbacks, collision checks, cliff/common transitions, and effect spawns.
Header prototype cleanup is not just cosmetic: replacing UNK signatures with Vec3, ItemLink, Fighter, and attribute pointers documents call contracts and reduces future reverse-engineering ambiguity.
Do not over-name unknown fighter motion vars prematurely; this PR used an explicit TODO for the newly added ftSamus_State7Vars because the proper state name was not yet known.
Address-based names remain acceptable for unmatched or not-yet-understood code, especially when combined with small semantic helper names around repeated behavior.
naming_conventions: Item-specific struct names use the existing pattern itSamusGrapple_ItemVars and itSamusGrappleAttributes.
Local semantic helpers use snake_case with a samus_grapple_ prefix, e.g. samus_grapple_anim and samus_grapple_state_sync.
Unresolved functions retain address-style names such as it_802B900C, it_802BA3BC, and fn_802B895C.
Motion callback wrappers keep existing names such as itSamusgrapple_UnkMotionN_Phys, indicating the motion/state semantics were not fully named.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style xNN names, but recovered types were applied where evidence existed, such as ItemLink*, s32, and f32.
The new ftSamus_State7Vars union member is deliberately named unk7 with a TODO for a proper state name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and explicit u8 padding locals to influence stack layout for near-matches.
Used static inline helpers to share repeated code while still allowing compiler inlining behavior needed for matching.
Preserved explicit if/else NULL assignments before HSD_JObjAddAnimAll rather than collapsing to terser expressions, likely to maintain codegen shape.
Used temporary scopes and variable shadowing, such as reloading Item* ip inside fn_802B7E34, to match original register/stack behavior.
Used explicit float constants and motion-ID comparisons, including the helper that checks motion IDs 0x165, 0x166, 0xD4, and 0xD6.
Matched HSD matrix setup flow with PSMTXIdentity, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, PSMTXConcat, HSD_JObjCopyMtx, flag writes, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty.
Collision helpers call mpColl_80041EE4, mpColl_SetECBSource_Fixed, mpCheckAllRemap, lbVector_Normalize, and lbVector_Mirror in ways that model grapple segment movement and reflection.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body/commentary says there are many near-matches and much duplicated code, and that semantic understanding of the grapple behavior would likely help organize the code and resolve more matches.
decomp.dev reported a positive matching result: matched code increased by 3336 bytes and matched data by 16 bytes, with 9 new matches and 13 improved unmatched items.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftSamus_State7Vars or itSamusgrapple_UnkMotionN_Phys to semantic state names.
Search subsequent itsamusgrapple.c changes for resolution of the near-matches listed by decomp.dev in PR #2328.
Compare this Samus grapple implementation with itlinkhookshot for reusable ItemLink chain, collision, and animation patterns.
Audit uses of xDD4_itemVar.linkhookshot versus xDD4_itemVar.samusgrapple to avoid union-member copy/paste mistakes.
Investigate the meaning of itSamusGrappleAttributes fields x0 through xA0 using article data and behavior-specific constants.
